name: Tauri
description: Webview-wrapper app shell; UI authored in HTML/JS, Rust provides IPC and system integration

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish of the `tauri` crate was Nov 27, 2019. The Tauri 1.0 stable release was June 2022; 2.0 stable was Oct 2, 2024.

maintenance-status.comment: Very active: frequent releases on the 2.x line, latest tauri 2.10.3 on 2026-03-04.

maturity.comment: 1.0 in June 2022, 2.0 stable in Oct 2024; large production user base.

corporate-backing.value: Tauri Programme / Commons Conservancy (non-profit foundation); CrabNebula as lead commercial partner
corporate-backing.comment: Governed as a Programme within the Dutch Commons Conservancy; sustained via sponsors and CrabNebula.

implementation.comment: Rust backend drives windowing (tao) and hosts the OS native webview (wry wrapping WebView2, WKWebView, WebKitGTK, Android System WebView). The UI itself is authored in HTML/JS/CSS via any JS framework the user picks.

rendering-mode.comment: Not meaningfully applicable: Tauri is an app shell, not a widget library. Rendering mode depends entirely on the frontend framework chosen by the user (React, Svelte, Leptos, plain HTML, etc.).

reactivity-model.comment: N/A at the Rust layer. Tauri exposes Rust commands via `#[tauri::command]` (an RPC-style callback mechanism), but the UI reactivity is whatever the chosen JS framework provides.

binding-target.comment: Binds to the OS-native webview: WebView2 (Windows), WKWebView (macOS/iOS), WebKitGTK (Linux), Android System WebView.

thread-model.comment: Windowing/webview runs on the main thread (OS constraint); Tauri owns a Tokio multi-threaded runtime for async commands and managed state is Send + Sync.

renderer.comment: Always renders via the OS-native webview; the actual GPU path (Skia/Metal/DirectX) is determined by that webview implementation, not by Tauri.

gpu-acceleration.comment: True indirectly: WebView2/WKWebView/WebKitGTK all use GPU compositing by default.

hidpi-support.comment: Inherited from the OS webview; all platform webviews are HiDPI-aware.

vector-rendering.comment: SVG, CSS vector primitives, and Canvas/WebGL via HTML.

custom-shaders.comment: No first-class Rust-side shader injection into the UI. WebGL/WebGPU shaders are usable inside the webview HTML layer.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: Tauri doesn't expose a native wgpu/OpenGL viewport inside the UI tree; any custom rendering happens via WebGL/WebGPU in the embedded webview.

macos.comment: macOS 10.15 Catalina and above; Apple Silicon and Intel.

linux-x11.comment: Linux via WebKitGTK; works under X11.

linux-wayland.comment: WebKitGTK runs under Wayland via GTK; officially supported.

ios.comment: Added in Tauri 2.0 (Oct 2024); iOS 9 and above.

android.comment: Added in Tauri 2.0 (Oct 2024); Android 7 and above.

web-wasm.comment: Not a target. Tauri is an app shell for native desktop/mobile; a web build would just be the underlying JS app without the Tauri runtime.

embedded-no-std.comment: Requires full std and an OS webview; no no_std or microcontroller story.

authoring-style.comment: N/A — UI is authored in HTML/CSS/JS via whatever frontend framework the user picks (React, Svelte, Vue, Leptos, plain HTML, etc.). Tauri itself is an app shell, not a widget-authoring framework.

hot-reload.comment: Hot-reload and HMR come from the frontend dev server (Vite etc.) via `build.devUrl`; Tauri 2 also extended HMR to mobile devices/emulators. Rust-side changes still require recompile.

layout-system.comment: N/A — layout is CSS-driven (flexbox, grid, etc.) via the webview, not provided by Tauri.

styling-approach.comment: Styling is plain CSS inside the webview. Using `css-like` tag because the UI is literally CSS; no Rust-side theming layer is shipped by Tauri.

theme-switching.comment: Windows can expose system theme preference and the webview picks up OS dark-mode; CSS-driven theme switching is trivial.

accessibility.comment: A11y is inherited from the OS webview's HTML/ARIA stack (same a11y surface as a browser). Tauri does not ship AccessKit integration; open issues report edge cases in frameless-window mode.

internationalization.comment: Full web-platform i18n (RTL, complex scripts, Intl APIs) via the webview; Tauri adds no i18n layer of its own.

widget-breadth.comment: N/A — Tauri ships no widgets. Widget breadth is whatever the chosen JS/HTML framework provides (HTML built-ins + component library).

data-grid-table.comment: Not provided by Tauri itself; users bring their own (AG Grid, TanStack Table, etc.) via the webview.

rich-text-markdown.comment: Not provided by Tauri; trivial to implement with HTML + a web markdown lib, but not a Tauri feature.

tree-outline.comment: Not provided by Tauri; supplied by the chosen frontend framework or component library.

native-menus.comment: Native menu bar and context menus are first-class in Tauri's Rust API (Menu, Submenu, MenuItem).

file-dialogs.comment: Official tauri-plugin-dialog provides native open/save file dialogs.

charts-plotting.comment: No first-party charting; users pick a JS charting library (Chart.js, D3, etc.).

custom-widgets.comment: N/A — custom widgets are authored in the chosen JS/HTML framework, not in Tauri itself.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Tauri owns and uses a Tokio multi-threaded runtime internally; async commands and app setup run on Tokio. Users can also hand Tauri their own Tokio Handle.

system-tray.comment: First-class TrayIcon API in the core crate.

drag-and-drop.comment: File drag-and-drop events are emitted on windows (see the `drag` example in the repo); HTML drag-and-drop inside the webview is supported as well.

embedded-webview.comment: The whole app *is* a webview; multiwebview (multiple webviews per window) is supported via a feature flag (see the `multiwebview` example).

multi-window.comment: Multiple top-level windows are a core feature; see the `multiwindow` example and the `windows` array in config.

binary-size.comment: Community reports put a minimal Tauri hello-world at roughly 2-3 MB bundle on macOS/Linux, fitting the 2-10 MB `small` bucket. Numbers vary by platform, frontend payload, and optimizations (LTO, strip).

startup-time.comment: Cold start dominated by webview initialization; typically 50-200ms on modern hardware for a minimal app. No rigorous independent benchmark referenced; bucket is a best-defensible estimate.

memory-footprint.comment: Idle RSS of a minimal Tauri app lands in the 20-80 MB range (webview + Rust runtime). Higher than pure-Rust immediate-mode frameworks but much lower than Electron.

idle-redraw.comment: Webviews repaint on demand via the browser compositor; no continuous 60fps redraw at idle.

dependency-count.comment: The `tauri` crate pulls in a large transitive graph (tao, wry, serde, tokio, many plugins); cargo tree on a default app easily exceeds 200 crates.

documentation-quality.comment: Full documentation site with guide, concepts, plugin docs, security model, end-to-end tutorials, API reference, and published books.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic Rust: `#[tauri::command]` attribute, builder API, derive macros, typed State injection. Minor friction from Mutex boilerplate and the large generated handler macro.

examples-count.comment: ~13-14 example apps in the repo (api, commands, drag, multiwindow, multiwebview, splashscreen, state, streaming, isolation, resources, helloworld, run-return, file-associations). Many more guides on the docs site.

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground — Tauri apps require a native build. The docs site hosts tutorials instead.

ide-tooling.comment: Official VS Code extension for tauri.conf.json validation and command palette; webview devtools act as the widget inspector. No Tauri-specific LSP (Rust code uses rust-analyzer, frontend uses its own tooling).

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Standard browser-style devtools are available inside the webview during development; covers DOM, network, console, performance.

github-stars.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-24; ~106k stars (growing); expect to stale quickly.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the `tauri` crate as of 2026-04-24.

notable-production-users.value: Hoppscotch, GitButler, Spacedrive, Cap, Yaak, Elasticvue, EcoPaste, and many shipping desktop/mobile apps on the awesome-tauri and madewithtauri lists

community-size.comment: Discord >20k members (17.7k at 2.0 launch, ~22k on invite page in 2026); broad contributor base; one of the most active projects in the Rust GUI space.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Large official plugin set (clipboard, dialog, fs, http, notification, shell, sql, stronghold, store, updater, tray, global-shortcut, autostart, deep-link, barcode-scanner, biometric, geolocation, nfc, haptics, ...) plus extensive community plugins and templates.
